text: 人的一切痛苦，本质上都是对自己的无能的愤怒。
author: 王小波

text: 生活不能等待别人来安排，要自己去争取和奋斗；而不论其结果是喜是悲，但可以慰藉的是，你总不枉在这世界上活了一场。
author: 路遥

text: 人是为了活着本身而活着，而不是为了活着之外的任何事物而活着。
author: 余华

text: 围在城里的人想逃出来，站在城外的人想冲进去，婚姻也罢、事业也罢，人生的欲望大都如此。
author: 钱钟书

text: 我慢慢地、慢慢地了解到，所谓父女母子一场，只不过意味着，你和他的缘分就是今生今世不断地在目送他的背影渐行渐远。
author: 龙应台

text: 世界上只有一种真正的英雄主义，那就是在认清生活真相之后依然热爱生活。
author: 罗曼·罗兰

text: 满地都是六便士，他却抬头看见了月亮。
author: 毛姆

text: 过去都是假的，回忆是一条没有归途的路，以往的一切春天都无法复原，即使最狂热最坚贞的爱情，归根结底也不过是一种瞬息即逝的现实。
author: 加西亚·马尔克斯

text: 每个人都是孤独的。我们生来就是一个人，最后也只能是一个人。但在孤独中，我们学会了与自己相处，这才是人生最重要的功课。
author: 蒋勋

text: 人生如逆旅，我亦是行人。
author: 苏轼

text: 读书多了，容颜自然改变，许多时候，自己可能以为许多看过的书籍都成了过眼云烟，不复记忆，其实他们仍是潜在的。在气质里，在谈吐上，在胸襟的无涯。
author: 三毛

text: 我们曾如此渴望命运的波澜，到最后才发现，人生最曼妙的风景，竟是内心的淡定与从容。
author: 杨绛

text: 人生有三大幸运：上学时遇到好老师，工作时遇到好师傅，成家时遇到好伴侣。
author: 季羡林

text: 无论走到哪里，都应该记住，过去都是假的，回忆是一条没有尽头的路。
author: 加西亚·马尔克斯

text: 一个人可以被毁灭，但不能被打败。
author: 海明威

text: 世界以痛吻我，要我报之以歌。
author: 泰戈尔

text: 我有一瓢酒，可以慰风尘。
author: 韦应物

text: 世间好物不坚牢，彩云易散琉璃脆。
author: 杨绛

text: 人生最大的幸福，是发现自己爱的人正好也爱着自己。
author: 张爱玲

text: 于千万人之中遇见你所要遇见的人，于千万年之中，时间的无涯的荒野里，没有早一步，也没有晚一步。
author: 张爱玲

text: 生命中真正重要的不是你遭遇了什么，而是你记住了哪些事，又是如何铭记的。
author: 加西亚·马尔克斯

text: 每一个不曾起舞的日子，都是对生命的辜负。
author: 尼采

text: 生活总是让我们遍体鳞伤，但到后来，那些受伤的地方一定会变成我们最强壮的地方。
author: 海明威

text: 一个人知道自己为什么而活，就可以忍受任何一种生活。
author: 尼采

text: 我们奋力向前，如同逆水行舟，不断地被浪潮推回到往昔岁月。
author: F·S·菲茨杰拉德

text: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife.
author: Jane Austen

text: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times, it was the age of wisdom, it was the age of foolishness.
author: Charles Dickens

text: All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: Happy families are all alike; every unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Whether I shall turn out to be the hero of my own life, or whether that station will be held by anybody else, these pages must show.
author: Charles Dickens

text: So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: Call me Ishmael. Some years ago—never mind how long precisely—having little or no money in my purse, and nothing particular to interest me on shore, I thought I would sail about a little and see the watery part of the world.
author: Herman Melville

text: The only thing necessary for the triumph of evil is for good men to do nothing.
author: Edmund Burke

text: Two roads diverged in a wood, and I—I took the one less traveled by, And that has made all the difference.
author: Robert Frost

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: I have measured out my life with coffee spoons.
source: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock
author: T.S. Eliot

text: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: The man who does not read has no advantage over the man who cannot read.
author: Mark Twain

text: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: The mystery of human existence lies not in just staying alive, but in finding something to live for.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: Many of the truths we cling to depend greatly on our point of view.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
source: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
author: Maya Angelou

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
author: John Lennon

text: What we call the beginning is often the end. And to make an end is to make a beginning. The end is where we start from.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: All animals are equal, but some animals are more equal than others.
author: George Orwell

text: There are darknesses in life and there are lights, and you are one of the lights, the light of all lights.
author: Bram Stoker

text: I took a deep breath and listened to the old brag of my heart: I am, I am, I am.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: 学而时习之，不亦说乎？有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎？
author: 孔子

text: 学而不思则罔，思而不学则殆。
author: 孔子

text: 三人行，必有我师焉。择其善者而从之，其不善者而改之。
author: 孔子

text: 道可道，非常道；名可名，非常名。
author: 老子

text: 上善若水，水善利万物而不争。
author: 老子

text: 人生天地之间，若白驹之过隙，忽然而已。
author: 庄子

text: 相濡以沫，不如相忘于江湖。
author: 庄子

text: 路漫漫其修远兮，吾将上下而求索。
author: 屈原

text: 天生我材必有用，千金散尽还复来。
author: 李白

text: 安能摧眉折腰事权贵，使我不得开心颜！
author: 李白

text: 无边落木萧萧下，不尽长江滚滚来。
author: 杜甫

text: 同是天涯沦落人，相逢何必曾相识。
author: 白居易

text: 大江东去，浪淘尽，千古风流人物。
author: 苏轼

text: 人有悲欢离合，月有阴晴圆缺，此事古难全。但愿人长久，千里共婵娟。
author: 苏轼

text: 醉里挑灯看剑，梦回吹角连营。
author: 辛弃疾

text: 众里寻他千百度，蓦然回首，那人却在灯火阑珊处。
author: 辛弃疾

text: 世事一场大梦，人生几度秋凉。
author: 苏轼

text: 人生自古谁无死，留取丹心照汗青。
author: 文天祥

text: 落红不是无情物，化作春泥更护花。
author: 龚自珍

text: 我劝天公重抖擞，不拘一格降人才。
author: 龚自珍

text: 君子之交淡如水，小人之交甘若醴。
author: 庄子

text: 但愿君心似我心，定不负相思意。
author: 李之仪

text: 衣带渐宽终不悔，为伊消得人憔悴。
author: 柳永

text: 独在异乡为异客，每逢佳节倍思亲。
author: 王维

text: 读书破万卷，下笔如有神。
author: 杜甫

text: 先天下之忧而忧，后天下之乐而乐。
author: 范仲淹

text: 老吾老以及人之老，幼吾幼以及人之幼。
author: 孟子

text: 鱼，我所欲也；熊掌，亦我所欲也。二者不可得兼，舍鱼而取熊掌者也。
author: 孟子

text: 人间有味是清欢。
author: 苏轼

text: To be, or not to be: that is the question.
author: William Shakespeare

text: All that glitters is not gold.
author: William Shakespeare

text: What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet.
author: William Shakespeare

text: The better part of valor is discretion.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Brevity is the soul of wit.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Love all, trust a few, do wrong to none.
author: William Shakespeare

text: The course of true love never did run smooth.
author: William Shakespeare

text: If music be the food of love, play on.
author: William Shakespeare

text: It is a wise father that knows his own child.
author: William Shakespeare

text: I am not bound to please thee with my answers.
author: William Shakespeare

text: It was the strife of passion and of principle, — the old, old story, as old as the hills.
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

text: Our doubts are traitors, and make us lose the good we oft might win by fearing to attempt.
author: William Shakespeare

text: I have a dream that one day on the red hills of Georgia, the sons of former slaves and the sons of former slave owners will be able to sit down together at the table of brotherhood.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.
author: Neil Armstrong

text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration.
author: Nikola Tesla

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge. Knowledge is limited. Imagination encircles the world.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The important thing is not to stop questioning. Curiosity has its own reason for existing.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity; and I'm not sure about the universe.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Art washes away from the soul the dust of everyday life.
author: Pablo Picasso
